content:
# Umm er Radhuma Formation

**Summary**: A Paleocene to Early Eocene carbonate formation.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] identified as the basal unit of the local sequence.

- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] studied the Umm Er Radhuma (UER) aquifer as the primary water source for the KFUPM campus.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] identified as a key geological unit in the Arabian Peninsula's sedimentary sequence.

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] investigated the umm-er-radhuma-formation for its potential to host karst features.

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] analyzed the Umm Er Radhuma formation to determine its role in the multi-aquifer system.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] analyzed the upward leakage of high-salinity water from the Umm Er Radhuma formation.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] studied the Umm Er Radhuma (UER) aquifer as the primary water source for the KFUPM campus.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] identified as a key geological unit in the Arabian Peninsula's sedimentary sequence.

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] investigated the umm-er-radhuma-formation for its potential to host karst features.

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] analyzed the Umm Er Radhuma formation to determine its role in the multi-aquifer system.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] analyzed the upward leakage of high-salinity water from the Umm Er Radhuma formation.

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Not stated in source ([[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]])

- The UER aquifer experienced a 480% increase in extraction between 1967 and 2010, leading to an average head drop of 8.5m. ([[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012, p. 15]])

- The UER formation consists of fossiliferous fine-grained recrystallized limestones with intercalated calcarenites and dolomites. ([[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018, p. 37]])

- The UER formation can reach a subsurface thickness of up to 500 m. ([[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018, p. 26]])

- The Umm Er Radhuma Formation is approximately 243m thick and contains a very fine to medium-grained yellowish gray dolomite. ([[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024, p. 33]])

- The isochlore map shows very high concentrations in excess of four times the concentration of seawater towards the coast. ([[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995, p. 103]])
